doc.name: Action
doc.description: Universal execution unit — follows a fixed pipeline: input → input_mapping → [execute] → output_mapping → output. The type determines HOW it executes: api (HTTP call), cli (shell command), function (registry lookup), event (bus dispatch), composite (chain), ui (JS handler). Universal mappings: input_mapping renames params before dispatch, output_mapping renames result after execution. Type-specific mappings: request_mapping and response_mapping apply inside api execution.

props.label: Type
props.description: Execution type: api (HTTP), cli (shell), function (registry), event (bus), composite (chain), ui (JS handler)

props.label: Target Class
props.description: Class this action applies to. Determines which objects can invoke this action.

props.label: Parameters
props.description: Input parameters schema — defines what the action accepts

props.label: Request Mapping
props.description: Source object field → request field mapping. Maps current object properties to action input parameters. e.g. {application_id: "app", environment_id: "env"}

props.label: Response Mapping
props.description: Response field → target object field mapping. Maps action output back to object properties. e.g. {status: "last_status", updated: "last_deploy_at"}

props.label: Provider
props.description: Provider that owns this action. Inherits base_url and auth from provider (type=api). Provider.actions[] is the canonical list.

props.label: Endpoint
props.description: URL path relative to provider base_url. Supports {field} placeholders from source object (type=api)

props.label: Headers
props.description: Additional HTTP headers as key:value object (type=api)

props.label: Input Mapping
props.description: Universal pre-dispatch key renaming. Applied to params BEFORE execution for all action types. Format: {from_key: to_key}. Unmapped keys pass through unchanged.

props.label: Output Mapping
props.description: Universal post-execution key renaming. Applied to result AFTER execution for all action types. Format: {from_key: to_key}. Unmapped keys pass through unchanged.

props.label: Command
props.description: Shell command template with {field} placeholders from source object (type=cli)

props.label: Working Directory
props.description: Directory to run command from. Supports {field} placeholders (type=cli)

props.label: Strategy
props.description: Execution strategy (type=composite): sequential=stop on fail, parallel=all at once

props.label: Confirm
props.description: Confirmation message before running (empty = no confirm)

doc.name: ES Application
doc.description: An application that uses elementStore — as server, client, or declaration provider

props.label: Role
props.description: How this app relates to elementStore

props.label: Server URL
props.description: ES API URL (serves or connects to)

props.label: Repositories
props.description: Repository IDs that interact with ES

props.label: Class Count
props.description: Total ES classes this app defines

props.label: CRUD Providers
props.description: CRUD provider IDs for API mapping

props.label: Client Language
props.description: Primary language of ES client code

props.label: Status
props.description: Current status of this app's ES integration

doc.name: App Feature Progress
doc.description: Tracks which ES features each application implements and their implementation progress

props.label: Application
props.description: The @app this feature record belongs to

props.label: Server Endpoints
props.description: HTTP endpoints this app exposes as a server for this feature (empty if client-only)

props.label: Implemented In
props.description: File paths (file:line) where this feature is implemented

doc.description: Top-level product or business unit grouping multiple repositories and services

props.label: Install Script
props.description: Path to shared one-time install script (relative to platform_root)

props.label: Docker Directory
props.description: Path to docker-compose stack relative to platform_root (e.g., docker/agura_wallet)

doc.name: Audit Entry
doc.description: Immutable audit record written after every object mutation (create, update, delete). Captures who made the change, when, and what changed. Never deleted — append-only audit trail.

props.label: Target Class
props.description: The class_id of the object that was mutated.

props.label: Object ID
props.description: The id of the object that was mutated.

props.label: Mutation Type
props.description: Type of mutation: create | update | delete.

props.label: User ID
props.description: ID of the authenticated user who triggered the mutation. Null for system/anonymous writes.

props.label: Agent ID
props.description: ID of the agent (AI or automated system) that performed the mutation. Null for direct user writes.

props.label: Session ID
props.description: Optional session/request correlation ID for grouping related mutations.

props.label: Signed At
props.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this audit entry was written (server time).

props.label: Changes
props.description: Field-level diff: {field_key: {old: ..., new: ...}}. Only populated when audit_include_changes is true on the active policy.

props.label: Policy ID
props.description: ID of the @signing_policy that matched this mutation.

doc.name: Change Set
doc.description: Represents a set of object changes broadcast via WebSocket. Each item contains the changed object data with _old holding previous values of changed fields.

props.label: Items
props.description: Changed objects with _old containing previous values

props.label: Sender
props.description: User ID that triggered the changes

props.label: Schema Hash
props.description: MD5 hash of class props at this version. Only present for @class changes.

doc.name: Class
doc.description: Defines a data class with its properties and behaviors

props.label: Storage
props.description: Storage provider (falls back to parent class, then default)

props.label: Abstract
props.description: Cannot create instances directly (only via child classes)

props.label: Contexts
props.description: Named view contexts (grid, form, compact, etc.) — assoc map of context_name → @class_context with field lists and actions

props.label: Icon
props.description: Default icon name for objects of this class (used in cards, lists, tabs)

props.label: Color
props.description: Accent color for this class (hex or CSS value, e.g. #6366f1). Used in cards, badges, navigation.

props.label: Providers
props.description: Data providers for external API integration

props.label: Unique Constraints
props.description: Field uniqueness constraints. Each entry: {id, fields[]}. The 'id' field is always unique by default. Additional constraints enforce uniqueness on field combinations.

props.label: Schema Version
props.description: Incremented on breaking schema changes. Objects track their version — on read, if object.version < class.version, normalizeObjectData casts to current schema.

props.label: Track Changes
props.description: When true (default), every setObject creates a @changes version record. Set false for classes that should not create version history (e.g. @changes itself, logs, temporary data).

props.label: External Links
props.description: Maps storage_id to external ID for provider-linked objects. Managed by ActionExecutor — never set manually.

props.label: Prompt
props.description: Markdown prompt describing class behavior, actions (setObject/getObject), execution flow, binding rules, and AI agent instructions. Used by AI agents to understand how to operate on objects of this class.

props.label: Unique Keys
props.description: Unique key definitions. Associative object keyed by name, each value is @key. Inherited by merge+override.

props.label: Actions
props.description: Executable actions on objects of this class. Called via PUT /store/{class}/{id}/{action}()

doc.name: Class Context
doc.description: Named view context for a class — defines which fields to show, sort order, and available actions. Inherited via extends_id (child overrides parent by context name).

props.label: Fields
props.description: Property keys to include (null = all visible props)

props.label: Page Size
props.description: Default page size for paginated views

props.label: Formatter
props.description: Display template with {{field}} placeholders for object label

props.label: Label
props.description: Which prop key is the primary display label

props.label: Children
props.description: Which prop key contains child items (for tree/menu)

props.label: Icon
props.description: Which prop key provides the icon

props.label: Filter
props.description: Default filter criteria for this context

props.label: Template
props.description: Visual template ID for this context: card-default, card-compact, card-media, list-item, tile, kanban-card

props.label: Title Field
props.description: Prop key to use as the primary title in card/list views

props.label: Subtitle Field
props.description: Prop key to use as secondary text / description in card views

props.label: Image Field
props.description: Prop key that provides an image URL or avatar for card/list rendering

props.label: Color Field
props.description: Prop key that provides an accent color (hex or CSS value) for the card/badge

props.label: Badge Field
props.description: Prop key to render as a status badge or chip in card/grid views

props.label: Grid Columns
props.description: Column definitions for grid/table contexts — ordered list of @visual_column declarations

props.label: Form Sections
props.description: Layout sections for form contexts — ordered list of @visual_section declarations with field groupings

doc.name: Cloudflare Provider
doc.description: Cloudflare API crud_provider. Extends @provider with Cloudflare-specific fields (zone_id, account_id). Supports DNS record creation/update and zone listing via REST API v4.

props.label: Zone ID
props.description: Default Cloudflare Zone ID used for DNS record operations. Can be overridden per action via params.

props.label: Account ID
props.description: Cloudflare Account ID. Used for account-scoped API calls.

doc.name: CloudWM Provider
doc.description: CloudWM (Kamatera) API crud_provider. Extends @provider with CloudWM-specific fields (datacenter). Supports server listing, creation, and power management via REST API. Authenticates with CWM_CLIENTID and CWM_SECRET.

props.label: Datacenter
props.description: Default datacenter code for server operations (e.g. EU, IL, US-NY2). Can be overridden per action via params.

doc.description: Docker port assignment

doc.name: Editor
doc.description: Defines a UI editor component for editing property values. Instances are seed data (text, textarea, select, etc.)

props.label: Default
props.description: Default editor for its data types

doc.description: Deployment environment
doc.name: Environment
Event
doc.description: Defines an event that can be subscribed to and triggered

doc.name: Exception
doc.description: Defines an exception type with HTTP code and a chain of actions to execute on the client. Actions are @action objects executed in order (log, show dialog, retry, redirect, etc.).

props.label: Error Code
props.description: Internal error code: not_found, forbidden, validation_failed, bad_credentials, etc.

props.label: Actions
props.description: Chain of @action objects to execute when this exception occurs (log, show_dialog, retry, redirect, etc.)

props.label: Retryable
props.description: Whether client can retry the failed operation

doc.prompt:
## @exception — Exception Handler

An @exception defines structured error handling for elementStore operations. Each exception has an error_code in PPPCEEE format (PPP=project, C=severity 8=critical/9=die, EEE=sequence) that uniquely identifies the error type.

Flow: When a StorageException is thrown, the error_code is looked up against registered @exception definitions. The matched definition specifies: the user-facing message template, severity level, whether to log, and optionally an action chain to execute for recovery or notification.

Client behavior: The client receives the error_code and message in API error responses. It can match on error_code to show contextual UI (e.g. validation_failed triggers field highlighting, not_found shows a missing-object dialog, forbidden shows access denied).

Action chain: An @exception can reference @action ids to execute on error — for example, logging to @log, sending a notification, or triggering a rollback composite action.

Validation errors: The special code validation_failed carries an errors[] array with per-field path, message, and code for granular UI feedback.

doc.name: ES Feature
doc.description: A canonical elementStore capability that can be implemented by clients and servers

props.label: Category
props.description: Broad classification of the feature area

props.label: Group
props.description: Feature sub-group for display organization within a category

props.label: Scope
props.description: Whether this feature applies to server, client, or both

props.label: Notes
props.description: Detailed logic description: classes, interfaces, actions, and how they interact

doc.name: Function
doc.description: Defines a reusable function — validators, transformers, generators, or computed fields

props.label: Scope
props.description: Which data types this function applies to

doc.name: Group
doc.description: Generic grouping class. Child classes set group_type as default+readonly to create typed groups.

props.description: Member objects — must be of a class listed in class_id_ref

doc.description: Deployment configuration linking an application and domain to its install/deploy scripts

props.label: Domain
props.description: Specific hostname for this installation (e.g., arc3d.master.local)

props.label: Deploy Script
props.description: Path from platform_root/ to the deploy.sh

props.label: Install Script
props.description: Path from platform_root/ to the install.sh (one-time setup)

props.label: CI/CD Trigger
props.description: Whether this deploy script is called by a CI/CD pipeline

props.label: SSH Host
props.description: SSH hostname for remote execution (empty = local)

props.label: SSH User
props.description: SSH user for remote execution (default: root)

props.label: Remote Platform Root
props.description: Absolute path to platform_root on the remote server

props.label: Install Steps
props.description: Ordered list of install components (clone_repos, create_symlinks, docker_stack, nginx_config, hosts, composer, health_check)

props.label: Sync Method
props.description: How env/ scripts are synced to the remote before running deploy actions. rsync=push from local; git-pull=remote pulls with deploy key; git-tunnel=SSH agent forwarding + git pull + clean deploy copy (no .git/docs); docker-build-push=build image + push to registry

props.label: Image Registry
props.description: Docker image registry URL (e.g. git.agura.tech:5050). Used with docker-build-push sync_method.

props.label: Image Registry Project
props.description: Registry project path (user/project, e.g. asi/mtms1). Used with docker-build-push.

props.label: Image Repo Host
props.description: Hostname/IP of the image repository server where tar.gz files are stored for k8s/remote access.

props.label: Image Repo User
props.description: SSH user for connecting to the image repository host.

props.label: Image Repo Path
props.description: Directory on the image repo host where tar.gz files are stored (e.g. /opt/image-repo/images/mtms).

props.label: Docker Services
props.description: List of service names to build and push (each must have a Dockerfile at docker_build_context/{service}/Dockerfile).

props.label: Docker Build Context
props.description: Path to the Docker build context directory. If relative, resolved from AGURA_DEV. If absolute, used as-is.

props.label: Docker Compose File
props.description: Path to docker-compose.yml for this installation. Relative to platform_root or absolute. Empty = no own compose stack.

props.label: Docker Env File
props.description: Path to the .env file for docker compose. Relative to platform_root or absolute. Created from .env.example if missing.

props.label: Docker Compose Build
props.description: Pass --build to docker compose up. Set true when source code is rsynced and images must be rebuilt.

props.label: Docker Network
props.description: Docker network that must exist before deploy (prerequisite check). Empty = no network check.

props.label: Nginx Container
props.description: Nginx container to deploy the per-installation nginx.conf into. Empty = no nginx config management.

props.label: PHP Container
props.description: PHP/worker container to restart on deploy (for installations without their own compose stack).

props.label: Health URLs
props.description: URLs to check for health status.

props.label: Hosts Entries
props.description: Hostnames that must be present in /etc/hosts. deploy_runner.sh adds them automatically during install.

props.label: Composer Repos
props.description: Repository IDs requiring composer install during install step.

props.label: Connect Containers
props.description: Container names to connect to docker_network on up (e.g. agura-auth).

props.label: Production Confirm
props.description: Require interactive 'yes' confirmation before up/install. Bypassed when CI_MODE=1.

props.label: Env Vars
props.description: Key-value pairs to write/override in the docker_env_file (.env) before running docker compose. Useful for per-installation path overrides (e.g. ARCHITECT_PATH=/var/www/cwm-architect).

props.label: Prerequisite Services
props.description: External Docker services that must be running before the main compose stack starts. deploy_runner.sh starts them automatically if not running. Each item: { container, image, health_url, network, volume, port, env_refs[] } — env_refs are key names resolved from env_vars at runtime.

doc.name: Key Definition
doc.description: Unique constraint on a class. fields[] is the composite key. Optional auto_field for auto-increment on the last field.

props.label: Fields
props.description: Array of prop keys forming the unique key

props.label: Auto Field
props.description: Which field in fields[] auto-increments (must be the last field)

props.label: Prefix
props.description: Prefix for auto-increment values (e.g. GRP-, INV-)

doc.description: System log entry — errors, warnings, info. Persisted in the store and auto-broadcast via WebSocket for real-time monitoring.

props.label: Source
props.description: Where the log originated (class, file, endpoint)

props.label: Context
props.description: Additional context — exception class, stack trace, request data

doc.name: Object Reference Value
doc.description:
Core system class — dynamic typed value whose type, editor, and validation are determined by a referenced @prop definition.

The ref field points to the @prop that defines the value's behavior:
- 'self': the @prop this @obj_ref belongs to (e.g., default_value on @prop uses the prop's own data_type/options)
- '@class.prop_key': a specific @prop by ID
- Any resolvable @prop reference

The editor resolves the ref at render time, reads the referenced @prop's data_type, options, and editor, then renders the value field accordingly. The server validates the value against the referenced @prop's type and constraints.

This is a core ES pattern for dynamic typing — used wherever a value's type is not fixed but determined by context (default values, template fields, computed results).

props.label: Reference
props.description: The @prop that defines how to edit and validate the value. 'self' = the @prop this obj_ref belongs to. Can also be a @prop ID like '@class.name'.

props.label: Value
props.description: The actual value — type is determined by the referenced @prop's data_type. Stored as raw JSON.

doc.name: DateTime Options
doc.description: Options for data_type: datetime — format, date range, granularity

doc.name: Number Options
doc.description: Options for data_type: integer/float — range, step, enum values

doc.name: Object Options
doc.description: Validation options for object/relation props — what values can be selected.

props.label: Fields
props.description: Which fields to expose/allow from the target class

props.label: Filter By
props.description: Filter conditions for available objects: {field: value}

doc.name: Relation Options
doc.description: Options for data_type: relation — foreign key binding, filtering, display, cascade behavior

props.label: Foreign Key
props.description: Which field on the related object points back to this one. E.g. on design.nodes: foreign_key='design_id' means baseElement.design_id points to this design. Determines parent-child binding.

props.label: Cascade Delete
props.description: When this object is deleted, also delete related objects. Default: false.

props.label: Display Field
props.description: Field to show as label (default: name)

props.label: Cascade Delete
props.description: When parent is deleted, also delete children. Default: false.

doc.name: String Options
doc.description: Options for data_type: string — enum values, length constraints, pattern validation

props.label: Allow Custom
props.description: Allow values outside the enum list

props.label: Pattern
props.description: Regex pattern the value must match

doc.description: Operational procedure
Platform project or sub-project

props.label: Path
props.description: Filesystem path relative to platform root

doc.name: Property
doc.description: Base property definition — common fields inherited by all @prop_* child classes

props.label: Data Type
props.description: Type of value — determines which @prop_* child class to use

props.label: Is Array
props.description: Multiplicity: false=scalar, indexed=ordered array, assoc=key-value map

props.label: Editor
props.description: UI editor widget — inline @editor instance with per-prop configuration. 'type' references the @editor definition, additional props are per-instance overrides.

props.label: Flags
props.description: Behavior flags: required, readonly, hidden, create_only, server_only, master_only

props.label: Default Value
props.description: Default value for new objects — type determined by this @prop's data_type

doc.name: Property Context
doc.description: Per-context override for a property — controls visibility, editability, label, editor, and display order within a named context (grid, form, compact, etc.)

props.label: Visible
props.description: Show this property in the context

props.label: Formatter
props.description: Display template: {{value}} or any {{field}} from the object

props.label: CSS Class
props.description: CSS class to apply to this field in this context

doc.name: Property Flags
doc.description: Boolean behavior flags for @prop — required, readonly, hidden, create_only, server_only, master_only, from_parent

props.label: Create Only
props.description: Only writable when creating new objects

props.label: From Parent
props.description: Value is auto-populated from parent object on create, reject direct writes on update, cascade on parent change. Value IS stored for queryability. Default: false.

doc.name: Number Property
doc.description: For integer and float data types

doc.name: Object Property

props.label: Strict Class
props.description: Only accept exact class match (not child classes)

props.label: Extensible
props.description: Allow extra keys beyond defined fields

props.label: Display Field
props.description: Field to show as label in UI (default: name)

props.label: Link Mode
props.description: How to resolve data: null=inline, embedded=stored IDs, foreign_key=reverse lookup, chain=walk parent

props.label: Link Field
props.description: Field name for foreign_key or chain walk (e.g. extends_id)

props.label: Merge Strategy
props.description: How to combine results: replace (default), override (assoc merge, child wins), append (concat)

props.label: Group By
props.description: Field to deduplicate by when merging (e.g. key for props)

props.label: On Delete
props.description: Action when referenced object is deleted

doc.description: Property holding inline object data. Structural props (object_class, extensible, link, merge) on root. Validation in options.

doc.name: Relation Property
doc.description: Pointer to another object. Extends @prop_object with locked defaults: extensible=false, link=embedded, on_delete=nullify.

doc.name: Provider
doc.description: Abstract base for data providers — defines how to connect to an external API. Extend this class to create concrete providers per external system. Use extends_id to inherit base_url and auth across multiple class-specific providers.

props.label: Description
props.description: What external system this provider connects to

props.label: Base URL
props.description: Base URL for API requests (inherited by child providers via extends_id)

props.label: Authentication
props.description: Auth config: {type: bearer|basic|apikey, token?, username?, password?, header?, key?}

props.label: External ID Field
props.description: Field name in API response that holds the external ID (stored in object._links)

props.label: Write Mode
props.description: crud=direct setObject allowed; actions_only=must use @action to modify linked objects

props.label: Field Mapping
props.description: Default API field → ES field mapping (overridden per action). e.g. {api_id: id, api_name: name}

props.label: Actions
props.description: Available actions on this provider (get_one, get_list, create_one, update_one, delete_one, + custom)

props.label: Default Parameters
props.description: Default query parameters added to every API request

doc.description: Base class for all platform registry entries
Git repository

props.label: Git URLs
props.description: SSH git URLs (primary first; may include mirrors e.g. GitHub)

props.label: Local Path
props.description: Local filesystem path to this repo. Use $AGURA_DEV/{repo_id} as the default convention. Override only when the repo lives outside AGURA_DEV. Supports $AGURA_DEV and $HOME variables.

props.label: ES Genesis
props.description: Paths to elementStore genesis/seed files relative to repo root. Empty = no elementStore integration.

props.label: ES Classes
props.description: Class IDs defined by this repository's genesis files. Used to detect cross-repo class overlaps and plan unification.

props.label: ES Base Classes
props.description: Root/abstract classes this repository's class hierarchy extends from. Shows architectural patterns.

doc.name: Seed
doc.description: Declarative data loading definition — classes to create and storage descriptors to load data from. Objects without class_id at root level are assumed to be @seed.

props.label: Classes
props.description: Class definitions to create or update

props.label: Seed Sources
props.description: Storage descriptors — files or URLs to load data from

props.label: Inline Objects
props.description: Objects to create directly (optional — prefer seed sources for large datasets)

doc.description: A running service or container within an application

props.label: Shared
props.description: Whether this service is shared across multiple applications

doc.description: Active user session. Created on login, destroyed on logout.
doc.name: User Session
Signing Policy
doc.description: Per-scope mutation signing configuration. Defines which identity fields are stamped on mutations and whether audit logging is required. One policy per class pattern; the default policy ('*') applies universally.

props.label: Class Pattern
props.description: Glob/regex pattern of class IDs this policy applies to. '*' = all classes. Examples: 'ai:*', '@action', 'user'.

props.label: Require User
props.description: Reject mutations that have no user_id in context. Enforces authenticated writes.

props.label: Require Agent
props.description: Reject mutations that have no agent_id in context. Enforces agent attribution.

props.label: Stamp created_by
props.description: Automatically stamp created_by (user_id) and created_by_agent (agent_id) on object creation.

props.label: Stamp updated_by
props.description: Automatically stamp updated_by (user_id) and updated_by_agent (agent_id) on every update.

props.label: Audit Enabled
props.description: Write an @audit_entry record for every matching mutation (create, update, delete).

props.label: Include Changes in Audit
props.description: Store the full field-level diff ({old, new} per changed field) in the audit entry. Disable for high-volume classes to reduce storage.

doc.name: Object State
doc.description: Server-managed state of every object. Read-only for clients.

doc.name: Storage
doc.description: Storage provider configuration — defines where and how class data is persisted. 'api' and 'composite' types proxy through @provider.

props.label: Type
props.description: Storage backend: local (browser), rest (ES API), api (external via @provider), seed (read-only), composite (multi-source), couchdb/mysql/json (server-only)

props.label: Provider
props.description: Data provider for external API integration (type=api)

props.label: Read Sources
props.description: Ordered storage IDs to read from (type=composite). Tried in order or merged.

props.label: Read Strategy
props.description: How to combine reads (type=composite): fallback=first hit wins, merge=combine all

props.label: Write Strategy
props.description: How to handle writes (type=composite): sequential=in order stop on fail, parallel=all at once, best_effort=all ignore failures

doc.name: Platform Tenant
doc.description: Tenant isolation unit.
Application URL mapped to project and environment(s)

doc.name: Current User
doc.description: Logged-in user context. GET /store/@user/me resolves to current user from JWT.

doc.name: Visual Column
doc.description: Grid/table column declaration — defines how a prop renders in a tabular context: width, render type, sort behavior.

props.label: Field
props.description: Prop key to display in this column

props.label: Label
props.description: Column header override (defaults to prop label if omitted)

props.label: Type
props.description: Render type: text, number, date, datetime, boolean, badge, avatar, link, icon, image, action, relation

props.label: Sortable
props.description: Whether this column supports sorting (default true)

props.label: Hidden
props.description: Hidden by default — user can toggle visibility

props.label: Formatter
props.description: Template string with {{value}} placeholder for custom cell rendering

doc.name: Visual Section
doc.description: Form layout section — groups fields under an optional heading with a configurable column grid.

props.label: Columns
props.description: Number of grid columns in this section (default 1)

props.label: Fields
props.description: Ordered prop keys to render in this section

doc.name: AI Agent
doc.description: WHO the agent is — identity, prompt, domain, tools. Runtime behavior in ai:agent_behavior via behavior prop.

props.label: Action Tools
props.description: @action IDs this agent can execute directly via es-action-tool.sh during its run

props.label: Role
props.description: Agent role code (e.g. CEO, CTO, DEV, QA, DEVOPS, coordinator, developer, reviewer)

props.label: Execution Layer
props.description: Execution layer this agent belongs to (e.g. leadership, foundation, build)

props.label: Boundaries
props.description: What this agent must NOT do (enforced by prompt)

props.label: Terminal Color
props.description: ANSI color code for terminal/UI identification

doc.name: Agent Behavior
doc.description: Runtime behavior config — session, triggers, approval, cooldown, budget

props.description: fresh=new each run, persistent=keep alive, timed=keep for session_ttl
Actions needing approval: code_changes, decisions, task_creation
When to run: round, task_assigned, question_received, schedule
What to do on agent failure

props.name: Self-Critique
props.description: F5: Run self-critique pass before TASK_COMPLETE

props.name: Max Context Tokens
props.description: F4: Max context tokens for this agent (default 8000)

doc.name: Agent Session
doc.description: A session belonging to an agent — type identifies the runtime (claude, openai, etc.)

props.description: External session ID (e.g. claude CLI session ID)

doc.name: AI Artifact
doc.description: F2 Typed Artifact — structured typed output produced by an agent during task execution. Agents emit ARTIFACT: <type> | <content> signals which are parsed and stored here.

props.name: Content
props.description: Artifact content (JSON string or plain text)

doc.name: AI Assistant
doc.description: Personal AI assistant bound to a user. Extends ai:agent with owner policy, preferences, working directories, and access control.

props.description: Owner user ID from auth service
Loop prompt — what the assistant does each iteration
Access policy: read_code, write_code, execute_bash, access_store, send_messages, create_tasks
Owner preferences: language, tone, verbosity, auto_commit, notify_on
Projects this assistant has access to
Claude Code config: permission_mode, allowed_tools, system_prompt_append, max_budget_usd

doc.name: Character
doc.description: Visual character with reactions for agent behavior

doc.name: Message Chunk
doc.description: Embedded chunk within ai:message — not independently stored

doc.name: Communication Provider
doc.description: A pluggable communication channel for agents. Each provider type knows how to poll/listen for messages and normalize them to ai:message format.

props.description: Connection settings: url, token_env, port, etc.
Auth config: token_env, api_key_env, user mapping
Provider capabilities: voice, files, reactions, threads
How to map provider messages to ai:message fields

doc.name: AI Conversation
doc.description: A thread between participants. Messages ordered by created timestamp. Linked to project, task, agent.
Clear all messages and reset the conversation
Close a currently open dialog
Show a confirm dialog — user must confirm or cancel before AI proceeds
Gracefully end and close the conversation
Fit all nodes in the viewport
Temporarily highlight nodes on the canvas
Update progress indicator for long-running operations
Request input from the user via inline prompt widget
Submit a response to an inline prompt-request widget
Select one or more nodes on the canvas
Send a user message to the conversation
Open a dialog or modal in the frontend
Abort the current streaming response
Show a toast notification in the UI
Zoom and pan the canvas to a specific element or area

doc.name: AI Task DAG
doc.description: F1 DAG Planning — directed acyclic graph of tasks with dependency tracking. Coordinator creates DAGs for complex multi-step work.

props.name: Tasks
props.description: All task IDs in this DAG

doc.name: Debug Entry
doc.description: Real-time debug trace entry — fire-and-forget via WebSocket, not persisted

props.description: Step payload — provider, tokens, params, results, errors

doc.name: Decision
doc.description: A recorded decision with rationale and assignment.

doc.name: AI Job
doc.description: Scheduled or triggered job — schema, events, and execution rules declared in object

doc.name: Agent Listener
doc.description: Input provider that an agent checks for incoming messages. Telegram, WhatsApp, ES messages, email, webhooks.

props.description: Last processed message ID for dedup
What to do when message arrives

doc.name: LLM Configuration
doc.description: LLM provider configuration with API keys

doc.name: AI Memory
doc.description: Persistent memory entry — generic data field for AI to store anything

props.description: Generic data payload — AI stores whatever it needs

doc.name: AI Message
doc.description: Universal message in the pool. One message can produce multiple results (questions, decisions, tasks, findings, approvals).

props.description: Primary creator — the message this belongs to. Child inherits context (task_id, user_id, agent_id, conversation_id) via from_parent flag.
Person who sent this (server-set from JWT, or system for AI)
Agents that should act on this
Array of results — each has result_type + type-specific fields. One message can produce multiple questions, decisions, tasks, etc.

doc.name: Preference
doc.description: A learned preference rule. Confidence grows with confirmation.

doc.name: Prompt
doc.description: A reusable prompt template with variable substitution.

doc.name: Prompt Request
doc.description: Generic user-input request from AI — rendered as inline widget in conversation. Types: confirm, alert, password, number, text, options

props.description: Unique request ID for matching response
Pre-filled value or default option index
Type-specific validation: { min, max, integer, pattern, minLength, maxLength, required }

doc.name: Prompt Response
doc.description: User response to an ai:prompt-request — includes confirm token for backend verification

props.description: User answer (stringified): true/false for confirm, number string, selected index, free text
Verification token: { timestamp: number, session_token: string }

doc.name: Prompt Proposal
doc.description: Agent-proposed improvement to a prompt file. Goes through CEO approval before being applied.

props.description: Which agent is proposing the improvement
The text to append to the prompt

doc.name: Question
doc.description: Agent question directed to agents or owner

doc.name: Approval Result
doc.description: Result shape when result_type=approval

props.description: ID of the message/question being approved

doc.name: Decision Result
doc.description: Result shape when result_type=decision

doc.name: Finding Result
doc.description: Result shape when result_type=finding

doc.name: Question Result
doc.description: Result shape when result_type=question

props.description: Role title: owner, all, security, etc.

doc.name: Task Result
doc.description: Result shape when result_type=task

doc.name: Round
doc.description: An execution round — one cycle of agents running sequentially.

doc.name: Task
doc.description: An actionable work item — assigned to agents, tracked through lifecycle.

props.name: Depends On
props.description: F1: Task IDs this task depends on (must complete first)

props.name: DAG
props.description: F1: ID of the parent ai:dag if this task is part of a DAG

props.name: Self-Critique Note
props.description: F5: Issue found during self-critique pass that blocked completion

props.name: Artifacts
props.description: F2: IDs of ai:artifact objects produced for this task

doc.name: AI Tool
doc.description: Tool that can be assigned to agents — binds to ElementStore classes and actions

props.description: Class+action bindings. Each entry: { type: 'class'|'class_action', ref_class_id: string, actions?: string[], action?: string, params?: object }

doc.name: AI Worker
doc.description: Running aic-api process — tracks PID, status, current round, session, and output log

doc.name: Credential
doc.description: Secure storage for API keys, tokens, secrets.

doc.name: Auth Module
doc.description: Feature module within an app. Groups permissions.

doc.name: Auth Permission
doc.description: Single permission: module + action. Grouped into roles.

doc.name: Auth Role
doc.description: Named role with permissions. Assigned to users per app.

doc.name: Team
doc.description: User team. Inherits @group with group_type locked to 'team'.

doc.name: Auth App Registration
doc.description: Credentials returned after registering this app with auth-service. One object per app_id. client_secret is server_only.

props.label: Auth Config
props.description: Auth config this registration belongs to

props.label: Client ID
props.description: Client ID assigned by auth-service after registration

props.label: Client Secret
props.description: Client secret — never exposed via API

props.label: Registered At
props.description: When this app was first registered

props.label: Active
props.description: Whether this registration is currently valid

doc.name: App Registration
doc.description: An application registered with the auth-service. Mirrors auth_app on the auth-service side.

props.label: Allowed Origins
props.description: CORS origins allowed to use tokens issued for this app

props.label: Scopes
props.description: Permission scopes this app is allowed to request

props.label: Active
props.description: Inactive apps are rejected at login

doc.name: Auth Config
doc.description: Auth-service connection configuration for this elementStore installation. One object per installation. All endpoint paths are configurable here.

props.label: App ID
props.description: Identifier for this app in the auth-service (e.g. 'elementStore')

props.label: App Name
props.description: Human-readable display name registered in auth-service

props.label: Auth Service URL
props.description: Base URL of the auth-service (e.g. http://agura-auth:3100)

props.label: Enabled
props.description: Enable auth enforcement on all API requests

props.label: Scopes
props.description: Permission scopes this app requests (e.g. read, write, admin)

props.label: Allowed Origins
props.description: Browser origins allowed to use tokens issued for this app

props.label: Verify Endpoint
props.description: GET — verify a token (remote check, optional)

props.label: JWKS Endpoint
props.description: GET — fetch public keys for local JWT verification

props.label: Register App Endpoint
props.description: POST — register this app with auth-service on first boot

props.label: Get App Endpoint
props.description: GET — check if app is already registered (appended with /{app_id})

props.label: List Machines
props.description: GET — list registered machines for this app

props.label: Revoke Machine
props.description: DELETE — revoke a specific machine registration

props.label: Public Key (cached)
props.description: Cached JWT public key fetched from JWKS endpoint at boot

props.label: Key ID (kid)
props.description: Key identifier from JWKS — used to select key for verification

props.label: Key Fetched At
props.description: When the public key was last fetched from auth-service

doc.name: Auth Machine
doc.description: Per-instance machine registration with auth-service. One object created/updated on each elementStore boot.

props.label: Auth Config
props.description: Auth config this machine belongs to

props.label: Instance ID
props.description: UUID generated once for this machine, persists across reboots

props.label: Machine ID
props.description: Machine ID returned by auth-service after registration

props.label: Hostname
props.description: Hostname of this machine at time of registration

props.label: IP Address
props.description: IP address of this machine at time of registration

props.label: ES Version
props.description: ElementStore version running on this machine

props.label: Registered At
props.description: When this machine was first registered

props.label: Last Boot At
props.description: Timestamp of the last successful boot registration

props.label: Active
props.description: Whether this machine is currently authorized

doc.name: Machine Registration
doc.description: A machine/instance registered under an app in auth-service. Mirrors auth_machine on the auth-service side.

props.label: Instance ID
props.description: UUID identifying this machine, generated on first boot

props.label: App Version
props.description: Version of the application on this machine

doc.name: Module
doc.description: A functional unit of an application that can be permission-gated (e.g. billing, reports, admin).

props.label: Name
props.description: Unique module key (e.g. billing, reports, elementstore.classes)

props.label: Description
props.description: What this module controls access to

props.label: App
props.description: Which application this module belongs to

props.label: Parent
props.description: Parent module for hierarchical permission trees

props.label: Actions
props.description: Available actions for this module (e.g. read, write, delete, admin)

props.label: Active
props.description: Inactive modules are ignored in permission checks

doc.name: Permission
doc.description: A rule granting a role access to specific actions on a module.

props.label: Module
props.description: Module this permission controls access to

props.label: Actions
props.description: Granted actions (subset of module.actions, or ['*'] for all)

doc.name: Refresh Token
doc.description: Issued long-lived refresh tokens. Used to obtain new access tokens without re-login. Stored for revocation.

props.label: User
props.description: User this token was issued to

props.label: App
props.description: Application the token was issued for

props.label: Machine
props.description: Machine this token was issued from

props.label: Token Hash
props.description: Hash of the actual token — never returned

props.label: Revoked
props.description: Revoked tokens are rejected even if not expired

props.label: Last Used At
props.description: Last time this token was used to refresh

doc.name: Role
doc.description: A named role that groups permissions. Assigned to users.

props.label: Name
props.description: Unique role name (e.g. admin, editor, viewer)

props.label: Default Role
props.description: Assigned to every new user automatically

props.label: Permissions
props.description: Module-action permissions granted to this role

doc.name: User
doc.description: A user account managed by auth-service

props.label: Display Name
props.description: Shown in UI; defaults to first + last name

props.label: Password Hash
props.description: bcrypt/argon2 hash — never returned by API

props.label: Email Verified
props.description: Whether the user has verified their email address

doc.description: A single match condition for an email rule. Multiple conditions combine via the rule's match_mode (AND/OR).

seed.name: Free
seed.description: Get started with elementStore — no credit card required. Perfect for personal projects and exploration.
seed.notes: No credit card required. 1 tenant, 5 user classes, 100 objects total. Designed to showcase the platform and convert to Pro.

seed.name: Pro
seed.description: For teams building real products. Unlimited classes, 10K objects, full API access, and priority support.
seed.notes: Price placeholder: $29/user/mo monthly, $23/user/mo annual (billed annually, 20% discount). Confirm pricing with owner before launch. 14-day free trial.

seed.name: Enterprise
seed.description: Custom deployment, SLA guarantees, SSO, dedicated support, and unlimited everything. Built for scale.
seed.notes: Custom pricing. Minimum contract TBD. Includes dedicated infrastructure option, custom SLAs, 24/7 support. Contact owner to define contract terms.
